political.s: Conagra maintains a bipartisan political stance through its Employee PAC, which supports candidates based on business objectives rather than party affiliation. The company raised approximately $61,000 in PAC contributions in the 2023-2024 cycle and spent $480,000 on lobbying in 2024, with FEC data showing a right-leaning distribution (32,500 to Republicans, 5,000 to Democrats from available data).

charity.s: Conagra demonstrates strong charitable commitment focused on food insecurity, having donated over 19 million pounds of food to Feeding America in 2025 and supporting 21 nonprofits through its Nourish Our Community grants program. Employee volunteerism exceeded 10,000 hours across 100+ organizations in 2025.
charity.amt: $19M+ food donations; Nourish grants distributed annually
charity.focus: Food insecurity, hunger relief, nutrition education, community development

environment.s: Conagra has established sustainability initiatives focused on climate, water, and waste reduction, with commitments to regenerative agriculture across supply chains and packaging innovation. The company joined the Circular Action Alliance and reported measurable progress in waste diversion and water efficiency improvements.

labor.s: Conagra has a mixed labor record with historical OSHA violations including safety lapses, though 32% of 33,000 employees are unionized. Recent concerns include overtime compensation lawsuits, but the company has negotiated settlements and implemented corporate-wide safety improvements.

dei.s: Conagra maintains pro-DEI stances with 100% scores on the Human Rights Campaign CEI, recognition as a Best Place to Work for Disability Inclusion, and active Employee Resource Groups. The company has set goals to double people of color in management by 2025 and achieve 40% women in management.

animals.s: Conagra sources animal-based ingredients with supplier standards aligned to its Animal Welfare Position Statement, emphasizing humane treatment. The company does not conduct animal testing but ensures compliance through supply chain partnerships.

guns.s: Conagra is a packaged food company with no involvement in firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: Conagra maintains comprehensive privacy policies compliant with CCPA, PIPEDA, and state privacy laws, with data deletion protocols and opt-out mechanisms. However, the company shares data with third-party vendors, ad networks, and service providers, presenting mixed privacy protections.

execPay.s: CEO Sean Connolly received $13.1 million in total compensation for fiscal 2025, with 89% tied to variable performance metrics. The CEO-to-worker pay ratio reflects significant executive compensation relative to average employee salaries of approximately $120,000.

fec.committeeName: CONAGRA BRANDS, INC. GOOD GOVERNMENT ASSOCIATION

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
